skill.name: Skywork Document
skill.description: Professional document generation skill for creating reports, proposals, blogs, papers, and business documents in multiple formats including docx, pdf, markdown, and html.
skill.summary: AI-powered document creation engine supporting multiple formats with web search integration and reference file processing.
skill.author: SkyworkAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 145 patterns across network, filesystem, environment access, and external command categories. All findings were evaluated as false positives. Network calls are to official Skywork API endpoints. Filesystem operations are for legitimate token storage and document download. External commands open browser for OAuth authentication. Environment variables are for configuration only. No malicious patterns, credential exfiltration, or data leakage detected.

low_findings.title: Network communication to external API
low_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to Skywork API endpoints for document generation and authentication. All endpoints are official Skywork domains.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Verified hardcoded URLs point to official api.skywork.ai and api-tools.skywork.ai domains used for legitimate API communication.

low_findings.title: OAuth token storage in home directory
low_findings.description: Authentication tokens are cached in ~/.skywork_token file for session persistence. This is standard practice for OAuth workflows.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Token file storage is implemented securely with JSON format and standard file permissions for user home directory.

low_findings.title: Browser launch via subprocess for OAuth
low_findings.description: The skill opens the default browser using subprocess.Popen to complete OAuth authentication flow. Commands are hardcoded per-platform.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Subprocess calls use hardcoded platform-specific commands (open, start, xdg-open) with no user input injection vectors.

content.value_statement: Creating high-quality documents requires time and expertise. This skill uses the Skywork Office Doc API to automatically generate formatted documents in multiple formats from your requirements and reference materials.
actual_capabilities: Create documents from scratch on any topic including reports, proposals, blogs, papers, and business documents
Process reference files (PDF, DOCX, XLSX) to base new documents on existing materials
Generate output in multiple formats: DOCX, PDF, HTML, and Markdown
Automatically perform web searches to gather current information for document content
Produce documents in multiple languages including English, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, French, German, and Spanish
Download generated documents to local machine with clickable cloud URL for sharing
limitations: Requires Skywork account authentication before first use
Document generation takes 5-10 minutes depending on complexity
Cannot be used for short plain-text answers or casual conversational replies
Reference files must be uploaded individually and parsed before document creation

use_cases.title: Business report generation
use_cases.description: Create comprehensive business reports, market research summaries, and analysis documents based on uploaded data files and requirements.

use_cases.title: Academic paper assistance
use_cases.description: Generate research summaries, study notes, and academic papers by processing reference materials and synthesizing content with web research.

use_cases.title: Professional documentation
use_cases.description: Produce proposals, contracts, memos, letters, and other business documents with professional formatting and structure.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple blog post
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a blog post about the benefits of remote work. Include an introduction, three main sections covering flexibility, productivity, and work-life balance, and a conclusion. Format as HTML.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Basic document creation without reference files

prompt_templates.title: Summarize a research paper
prompt_templates.prompt: Based on the uploaded PDF file, create a 2-page summary document that captures the main research question, methodology, key findings, and implications. Write in clear English suitable for a general audience.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - Document creation with reference file processing

prompt_templates.title: Generate a market research report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a comprehensive market research report on the electric vehicle charging industry in Brazil. Include market size, growth trends, key players, regulatory environment, and future opportunities. Use the attached Excel data file for statistics. Format as a professional Word document with charts and tables.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - Multi-file analysis with structured output

prompt_templates.title: Translate and adapt a document
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate the attached English business proposal into Simplified Chinese while adapting the tone and examples for a Chinese business audience. Maintain the original structure and key points but localize cultural references.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - Translation and localization with reference materials

output_examples.input: Create a one-page memo announcing the new remote work policy to all employees.
Summarize the attached quarterly sales report into an executive summary document.
output: The document includes key metrics, regional performance highlights, and recommendations for Q1.

best_practices: Always run the authentication script (skywork_auth.py) before first use to establish your Skywork session
Parse all reference files before calling create_doc.py to obtain file IDs for the document generation request
Provide detailed content prompts that specify structure, sections, tone, and key points for best results
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for short plain-text answers or simple Q&A that does not require formatted output
Do not skip authentication or attempt to call document scripts without a valid token
Do not paste large amounts of parsed file content manually - the server retrieves content automatically using file IDs

faq.question: How do I authenticate with Skywork for the first time?
faq.answer: Run the skywork_auth.py script. It will check for existing tokens, and if none are found, open your browser to complete login. The token is then cached for future use.

faq.question: What file formats can I use as references?
faq.answer: The parse_file.py script supports PDF, DOCX, XLSX, and other common document formats. Each file must be parsed individually before document creation.

faq.question: How long does document generation take?
faq.answer: Typical generation time is 5-10 minutes depending on document complexity. The script shows real-time progress and waits automatically for completion.

faq.question: Can I generate documents in languages other than English?
faq.answer: Yes. The --language parameter supports English, Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, Japanese, Korean, French, German, Spanish, and more.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports DOCX (Word), PDF, HTML, and Markdown formats. Specify using the --format parameter or default to DOCX.

faq.question: What happens if I receive an insufficient benefit error?
faq.answer: This means your Skywork account needs to be upgraded. The error message includes a link to the upgrade page. Click the link to complete the upgrade and try again.
